cta.title: Take the Next Step Toward Natural Wellness
cta.subtitle: A consultation is a great place to start. Together, we'll explore which treatments align with your health goals and build a personalized care plan designed around your individual needs.

faqs.answer: Acupuncture is generally well-tolerated, with most people experiencing little to no discomfort. Because the needles are hair-thin — far finer than the needles used for injections — insertion tends to feel like a brief, mild sensation at most. After placement, patients often settle into a state of deep relaxation, and it's quite common to drift off to sleep during the session.
faqs.question: Does acupuncture hurt?

faqs.answer: Treatment frequency depends on several factors, including your specific condition, how long you've been dealing with it, and how your body responds over time. Acute conditions may show improvement within 3–6 sessions, whereas chronic concerns often call for 10–15 treatments or more. We'll put together a personalized treatment plan from the start and continue to refine it as we track your progress.
faqs.question: How many sessions will I need?

faqs.answer: When practiced by a qualified, licensed practitioner, Chinese medicine is considered safe. We use sterile, single-use needles and adhere to rigorous safety protocols throughout every treatment. Chinese herbal medicine has been developed and refined across thousands of years of practice, and we source only high-quality, tested herbs from reputable suppliers. Side effects, when they do occur, are uncommon and typically minor.
faqs.question: Is Chinese medicine a safe option?

faqs.answer: Yes — TCM and Western medicine can work well together. Many patients turn to acupuncture to help manage side effects from medications or medical procedures, to support recovery, or to address concerns that conventional treatments haven't fully resolved. We strongly encourage open communication with all of your healthcare providers so that your care remains coordinated and consistent.
faqs.question: Can I use TCM alongside Western medical care?

faqs.answer: Loose, comfortable clothing works best — choose items that can be easily rolled up to allow access to your arms, legs, and back. Gowns are available if you need them. We also ask that you skip perfume or heavily scented products on treatment days, as some patients may have sensitivities.
faqs.question: What should I wear to my treatment appointment?

faqs.answer: We work with most major insurance plans that provide acupuncture coverage. If your plan requires direct submission, we can provide a detailed superbill for you to send to your insurer for potential reimbursement. We recommend contacting us ahead of your visit to confirm your specific benefits. For those paying out of pocket, we offer accessible self-pay rates as well as package pricing options.
faqs.question: Does your practice accept insurance?

faqs.answer: Have a light meal one to two hours before your visit — arriving on an empty stomach isn't recommended. On treatment days, try to limit alcohol and excess caffeine. Bring a list of any medications or supplements you're currently taking, and wear comfortable clothing. Plan to share your health history in some detail, and feel free to come with questions.
faqs.question: How do I prepare for my first appointment?

faqs.answer: Needle anxiety is more common than you might think, and many patients are genuinely surprised by how comfortable acupuncture feels. The needles are extremely fine, and insertion is typically painless. We'll move at a pace that feels right for you, walk you through each step, and can begin with fewer needles if that helps you feel more at ease. We also offer needle-free options, including herbal medicine, cupping, and Tui Na massage.
faqs.question: What if needles make me nervous?

faq.title: Your Questions, Answered
faq.subtitle: Helpful answers about our services, safety practices, and what you can expect from care

hero.title: Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) Modalities
hero.subtitle: A full range of healing therapies, thoughtfully adapted to your individual health needs

overview.title: What Our Care Model Supports
benefits: Support for stress and anxiety through natural approaches
Greater energy and a renewed sense of vitality
Digestive health and gut wellness support
Support for injury recovery and rehabilitation
overview.introduction: Dr. Henry Smith Clinic provides a full spectrum of Traditional Chinese Medicine treatments, each one carefully chosen and adapted to your specific health concerns and wellness goals. Our approach brings together time-honored practices and contemporary understanding, offering natural, whole-person care that addresses the root of your concerns — not just the symptoms.

items.title: Licensed Practitioners
items.description: Care grounded in evidence-informed clinical standards

items.title: Individualized Treatment Plans
items.description: Every plan is shaped around your personal health needs and goals

items.title: Safe & Professional
items.description: Rigorous hygiene and professional treatment standards throughout

items.title: Acupuncture
benefits: Pain relief that doesn't rely on medication
items.whatToExpect: Your acupuncture session begins with you settling into a comfortable position while your practitioner gently places hair-thin needles at carefully chosen points. The vast majority of patients notice little to no discomfort. For the next 20 to 30 minutes, you simply rest as the needles do their work — and a deep sense of relaxation often follows. It's not uncommon for patients to drift off to sleep!
items.fullDescription:
Acupuncture works by stimulating the body's meridians through the precise placement of needles, supporting the balance of Yin and Yang and encouraging the healthy flow of Qi and blood. 

**Scope of Application**
Acute and chronic pain conditions (including neck, shoulder, back, and leg pain, headaches, and arthritis), internal medicine concerns (such as insomnia, gastrointestinal disorders, and hypertension), surgical and soft tissue issues (including soft tissue injuries and skin conditions), gynecological concerns (such as irregular menstruation and menopausal syndrome), pediatric conditions (including indigestion and low immunity), men's health (including prostate enlargement, impotence, premature ejaculation, and sexual dysfunction), and aesthetic TCM applications (including acne management, anti-aging, weight management, skin lifting, and edema reduction). 

**Precautions**
Please avoid receiving acupuncture on an empty stomach or immediately after a large meal. Those who are pregnant or living with severe heart disease or bleeding disorders should exercise caution. Let your acupuncturist know if you are taking any anti-platelet or anticoagulant medications. After your session, stay warm, avoid cold drafts, and allow at least 4 hours before bathing.
items.shortDescription: A cornerstone of our care, acupuncture uses fine needles to restore the body's natural balance and support its capacity to heal.

items.title: Chinese Herbal Medicine
benefits: Tailored specifically to your individual health needs
Focuses on underlying causes rather than surface symptoms
Generally well tolerated with minimal side effects
Suited to managing ongoing and chronic conditions
Aids in maintaining healthy internal organ function
items.whatToExpect: Your journey begins with a thorough consultation and TCM diagnosis. From there, we develop a herbal formula designed around your unique needs, and you'll leave with clear guidance on how to prepare and take your herbs. As your treatment progresses, we check in regularly and refine the formula to keep your care on track.
items.fullDescription:
Chinese Herbal Medicine applies carefully selected herbal formulas to harmonize the Yin, Yang, Qi, and blood of the organs, guided by TCM syndrome differentiation. 

**Scope of Application**
Internal medicine conditions (including stomach complaints, liver disease, and cardiovascular disease), gynecological concerns (such as menstrual disorders, infertility, and menopause), pediatric issues (including colds and indigestion), men's health (including kidney deficiency and impotence), recovery from chronic illness, and anti-aging and wellness support. 

**Precautions**
Herbal formulas must always be used under the guidance of a qualified TCM practitioner. If you are currently taking Western medications, please inform your practitioner so that the herbal prescription can be adjusted appropriately. Pregnant women and those in special health circumstances must follow their practitioner's advice closely.
items.shortDescription: Individually crafted herbal formulas designed to address the root of your health concerns and support your body's own healing process.

items.title: Cupping Therapy
items.whatToExpect: Your practitioner places glass or silicone cups over areas of tension or discomfort. Rather than pain, most people describe the suction as a satisfying pulling sensation. Cups may stay in one spot for 5 to 15 minutes or be glided across the skin. Temporary circular marks can appear afterward — this is completely normal and typically fades within a few days.
items.fullDescription:
Cupping therapy applies negative pressure to draw cups firmly against the skin, expanding the capillaries beneath to encourage blood flow, clear stagnation, and help the body expel wind and cold. 

**Scope of Application**
Colds, coughs, asthma, rheumatic joint pain, and muscle tension in the neck, shoulders, and back arising from wind-cold pathogens. 

**Precautions**
Cupping is not appropriate over broken or irritated skin, for those with bleeding disorders, or over the abdominal and lower back areas of pregnant women. Elderly patients should proceed with care. Following treatment, protect yourself from cold exposure, avoid overexertion, and wait before bathing to prevent wind-cold from entering through the pores.
items.shortDescription: A time-honored therapy that uses suction cups to ease muscle tension, encourage better circulation, and support the body's natural recovery.

items.title: Moxibustion
benefits: Helps restore energy levels and a sense of vitality
items.whatToExpect: Throughout your moxibustion session, you'll notice a soothing warmth spreading from the burning moxa as it's held close to targeted points on your body. The experience is grounding and deeply relaxing — many patients liken the sensation to the gentle warmth of sunlight on their skin. The moxa herb also carries a distinctive, earthy scent that many find calming.
items.fullDescription:
Moxibustion harnesses the therapeutic warmth produced by burning moxa leaves to activate specific acupuncture points, warm the meridians, and nourish the body's vital energy. 

**Scope of Application**
Deficiency-cold constitutions, menstrual cramps, chronic gastrointestinal conditions, rheumatic pain, asthma, and general fatigue or cold extremities. 

**Precautions**
Care should always be taken to avoid burns during treatment. Moxibustion is not appropriate for individuals with high fever or constitutions characterized by excess heat. People with diabetic neuropathy should exercise extreme caution. Pregnant women should avoid application to the abdominal and sacral regions.
items.shortDescription: A gentle warming therapy using dried mugwort to nourish vitality, support circulation, and help the body dispel cold.

items.title: Tui Na Medical Massage
benefits: Eases muscle pain and chronic tension
Encourages improved posture and structural alignment
May help reduce the frequency and intensity of headaches and migraines
items.whatToExpect: Depending on your condition and constitution, Tui Na can feel anywhere from gentle and soothing to more active and invigorating. Your practitioner applies a range of techniques using their hands, fingers, elbows, and occasionally feet. You stay fully clothed throughout, and a typical session runs between 30 and 60 minutes.
items.fullDescription:
Tui Na applies skilled manual techniques to the body's meridians, muscles, and joints, working to harmonize Qi and blood, release tension in the tendons, and improve circulation in targeted areas. 

**Scope of Application**
Neck, shoulder, back, and leg pain, muscle strain, sports injuries, indigestion, and pediatric conditions such as cough or constipation. 

**Precautions**
Tui Na should not be performed over open wounds, active skin infections, or areas affected by bone tumors. Deep pressure on the abdomen is not appropriate for pregnant women. When treating elderly patients or those with osteoporosis, only gentle techniques should be applied.
items.shortDescription: A traditional Chinese medical massage that blends acupressure, assisted stretching, and joint mobilization into one therapeutic approach.

items.title: Gua Sha
benefits: Relief from neck and shoulder tension
items.whatToExpect: Your practitioner begins by applying oil to the skin, then uses a smooth-edged tool to work over areas of tension or discomfort with firm downward strokes. The sensation involves noticeable pressure and scraping — it can feel strong, though most people find it quite manageable. Redness or purple-toned marks often appear on the skin during the session and generally fade within 2 to 7 days.
items.fullDescription:
Using a smooth scraping tool applied with friction, Gua Sha works to stimulate circulation, clear the meridians, and draw stagnant blood toward the surface to help the body expel pathogens. 

**Scope of Application**
Commonly used for colds, fever, sore throat, heatstroke, hypertension, and tension or stiffness in the neck, shoulders, and back. 

**Precautions**
Do not apply over broken skin or moles. This therapy is not appropriate for individuals with bleeding disorders, and should not be performed on the abdomen during pregnancy. After treatment, keep the area warm and covered, and avoid cold air drafts or bathing right away.
items.shortDescription: A time-honored scraping technique that helps ease muscle tension, support circulation, and reduce inflammation.

items.title: Herbal Plaster Therapy
items.fullDescription:
Custom-prepared herbal pastes or powders are applied directly to targeted acupoints or painful areas, allowing medicinal compounds to be absorbed through the skin. 

**Scope of Application**
Widely used for rheumatic joint pain, persistent waist and leg pain, asthma, rhinitis, pediatric cough conditions (such as San-Fu-Tie), and a range of skin concerns. 

**Precautions**
Exercise caution when applying to sensitive skin. Do not use on broken skin or open wounds. If significant redness, swelling, or itching develops, discontinue use right away.
items.shortDescription: Individually tailored nutrition guidance grounded in TCM principles, designed to support your healing process and help restore balance.

items.title: Lifestyle & Wellness Counseling
benefits: Greater energy and a sense of vitality
Proactive support for reducing future illness
items.whatToExpect: As part of your care, we take time to explore different areas of your daily life and offer clear, practical guidance you can act on. Recommendations may cover topics such as breathing exercises, meditation practices, healthy sleep habits, adjusting routines with the seasons, and approaches to handling stress. Our goal is to work alongside you in building changes that feel realistic and lasting.
items.fullDescription:
Lasting wellbeing involves more than in-clinic treatments alone.

Our comprehensive lifestyle counseling looks at the full picture — including your sleep patterns, physical activity, stress management approaches, and everyday habits.

Every recommendation draws on TCM principles and is thoughtfully shaped around your individual constitution and personal circumstances.
items.shortDescription: Whole-person guidance covering sleep, movement, stress, and daily routines — all in support of your ongoing wellness journey.
